reasoning: The current high yield spread of 3.25 (as of March 14, 2025) represents a recent spike up from the ~2.81 baseline in late February. Similar spikes historically (like August 2024 when it hit 3.49) tend to mean-revert quickly over the following weeks and months, dropping back to the ~2.90-3.10 range. The spread has already started declining (dropping from 3.40 on March 13 to 3.25 on March 14), signaling that the short-term shock is unwinding. Therefore, the probability of the spread increasing above its March 16 value (anchored around 3.25) across all horizons is systematically lower than 50%. Following statistical guidelines, uncertainty strictly increases with the forecast horizon, pulling long-term probabilities steadily closer to the 50% baseline.
